mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with low unemployment indicates a tightening labor market, which drives corporate profit margins and stock valuations higher, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Jointly high PAYEMS and low UNRATE fail to produce positive 20-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger real economic activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns positively over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO changes (Rank IC < 0.05) across all subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect elevated consumer demand for durable goods, which drives equity market optimism and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high HOUST regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or show negative correlation with HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% triggers central bank rate hikes, which increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive despite CPIAUCSL above 2%, or the signal's Rank IC is negative in inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and retail sales suggests robust economic expansion, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in unemployment above 5% signals economic distress, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive despite UNRATE above 5%, or the signal's Rank IC is negative in high-unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment indicates economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 returns negatively over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE fails to produce negative returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.10).
